tools.name: inspect_cdm_json
tools.description: Analyze CDM JSON structure. Returns: tree, enums_used, type_summary, type_registry (Java imports/builders per schema ref), java_type_warnings (date etc.), well_known_imports (common CDM/JDK types to import), reference_pattern_total + reference_patterns_sample + reference_api_note when globalReference/externalReference appear (use setGlobalReference/setExternalReference/setReference; never setAddress). Call FIRST — use type_registry directly instead of resolve_java_type for each type.

json_path.description: Path to the CDM JSON file to analyze

tools.name: lookup_cdm_schema
tools.description: Look up the JSON Schema definition for a CDM type. Returns properties with types, $ref, required flags, Java classes, setter_hint, optional setter_note (e.g. JSON address → setReference, not setAddress), and builder_reference_note on ReferenceWithMeta* types.

type_name.description: CDM type name (e.g. 'Trade', 'Party', 'SettlementPayout') or schema filename

tools.name: resolve_java_type
tools.description: Resolve a CDM schema $ref to its fully-qualified Java class name, package, builder class, and import statement. Use this when you need the exact import for a type referenced in a schema.

tools.name: list_enum_values
tools.description: List enum JSON values with java_constant names (uses schema oneOf titles when present; otherwise sanitized identifiers). May include enum_constant_warning for punctuation-heavy values — verify against JAR if compile fails.

tools.name: get_java_template
tools.description: Get the boilerplate Java file template with main method and RosettaObjectMapper serialization. Contains IMPORTS_PLACEHOLDER and BUILDER_CODE_PLACEHOLDER markers for you to fill in. The public class name matches the active generation target (derived from the CDM JSON filename stem for this run).

tools.name: write_java_file
tools.description: Write complete Java source code to the generation output directory. Overwrites any existing file.

code.description: Complete Java source code to write

filename.description: Output filename (omit to use active target, e.g. MyTrade.java from CDM JSON stem)

tools.name: read_java_file
tools.description: Read the current content of the generated Java source file. Use this to review code before compilation or after making patches.

filename.description: Filename to read (omit to use active generation target)

tools.name: patch_java_file
tools.description: Replace text sections in the Java file. Supports single replacement (old_text/new_text) or batch mode via the patches parameter (list of {old_text, new_text} pairs). Use batch mode to apply multiple independent fixes in one call.

old_text.description: Exact text to find in the file (for single replacement)

filename.description: Filename to patch (omit to use active generation target)

patches.description: Batch mode: list of {old_text, new_text} pairs to apply in one call

tools.name: compile_java
tools.description: Compile the Java file against the CDM classpath (rosetta-validator fat JAR). Returns structured compilation errors with line numbers, messages, and the offending source line. If successful, the .class file is ready to run.

filename.description: Java source file to compile (omit to use active generation target)

tools.name: run_java
tools.description: Execute the compiled Java class. Captures stdout (expected to be CDM JSON) and stderr. The class should have a main method that builds a Trade, serializes it via RosettaObjectMapper, and prints it.

class_name.description: Class name to run (omit to use active generation target)

tools.name: validate_output
tools.description: Validate the Java-produced JSON against the official CDM Trade JSON Schema (845 schemas with $ref resolution). Confirms the output is structurally valid CDM.

tools.name: finish
tools.description: Signal that the task is complete. Call this when the Java code compiles, runs, and produces reasonable CDM JSON on stdout (or when max iterations are reached).

java_file.description: Path to the generated Java file

match_percentage.description: Optional self-reported quality score (0-100); not computed automatically
